question_text: A student is investigating different types of electromagnetic radiation. She measures that radiation A has a wavelength of 700 nm and radiation B has a wavelength of 0.1 nm. Calculate the frequency of radiation B if the speed of light is 3 × 10⁸ m/s, and explain which type of electromagnetic radiation B is likely to be.
correct_answer: Frequency = 3 × 10¹⁸ Hz; Radiation B is X-rays (or gamma rays)
explanation: To find frequency, use f = c/λ. Converting 0.1 nm to metres (0.1 × 10⁻⁹ m), then dividing the speed of light by this wavelength gives 3 × 10¹⁸ Hz. This extremely high frequency corresponds to X-rays, which have very short wavelengths and high energy.
